results.title: variables-scoping
results.description_long:
# Variables â€” Scoping & Access Modes

Exercises the two orthogonal dimensions of `node_variables`:

1. **`scope`** â€” where a variable is visible across the workflow graph:
   `workflow` (global), `node` (local to its origin), `specific_nodes`
   (explicit allowlist via `scope_node_ids`).
2. **`access_mode`** â€” how the runtime may read/write the variable:
   `settable` (LLM extracts from user input), `read_only` (pre-populated,
   rendered into prompts/tts but never re-written), `private` (never
   rendered, never leaked to the LLM or transcript).

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `global_scope_persists` | `var {name} scope=workflow â†’ visible on downstream {node}` |
| `mixed_access_modes` | `one node Â· {private} + {read_only} + {settable} vars coexist` |
| `mixed_scopes` | `one node Â· {workflow} + {node} + {specific_nodes} scopes coexist` |
| `node_scope_isolated` | `var {name} scope=node â†’ invisible on downstream {node}` |
| `private_variable` | `var {name} access_mode=private â†’ never rendered, never extracted` |
| `read_only_variable` | `var {name} access_mode=read_only â†’ renders via {{ jinja }}, not extracted` |
| `settable_variable` | `var {name} access_mode=settable â†’ extracted by LLM` |
| `specific_nodes_scope` | `var {name} scope=specific_nodes ids=[{n1},{n2}] â†’ visible only on listed {node}` |

results.title: steps-transfer
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Transfer

Exercises `PhoneRoutingNode` terminal transfer with a `goodbye_message`
spoken before the call is routed. Both scenarios share an identical
`CollectIntent` LLM entry node and differ only in the transfer node's
`goodbye_message_mode`.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `transfer_with_flexible_goodbye` | `transfer with paraphrased goodbye (mode: flexible)` |
| `transfer_with_say_exactly` | `transfer with literal goodbye (mode: exact)` |

results.title: steps-spellout
results.description: `collect â†’ {var} with confirmation_mode: explicit_spellout` across data types
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Spellout Modes

Exercises the `explicit_spellout` value on `node_variables[].confirmation_mode`.
When a `collect` step targets a variable with this mode, the bot reads the
captured value back character-by-character (or digit-by-digit / grouped,
depending on type) and waits for an affirmative user confirmation before
transitioning out of the node.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `explicit_spellout_boolean` | `collect â†’ opted_in (Boolean Â· explicit_spellout)` |
| `explicit_spellout_date` | `collect â†’ check_in_date (Date Â· explicit_spellout)` |
| `explicit_spellout_email` | `collect â†’ email (Email Â· explicit_spellout)` |
| `explicit_spellout_email_invalid_correction` | `collect â†’ email (Email Â· explicit_spellout) with invalid correction â†’ pending cleared` |
| `explicit_spellout_email_invalid_then_valid` | `collect â†’ email (Email Â· explicit_spellout) with pre-confirm validation_error` |
| `explicit_spellout_numeric_code` | `collect â†’ zip_code (NumericCode Â· explicit_spellout)` |
| `explicit_spellout_phone` | `collect â†’ phone_number (PhoneNumber Â· explicit_spellout)` |
| `mixed_implicit_and_explicit_spellout` | `collect â†’ {name (implicit), email (explicit_spellout)} in one node` |

results.title: steps-speak
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Speak

Exercises every flavor of utterance-producing instruction step inside a
task-following LLM node: `speak` (`exact` and `flexible`), `custom_prompt`
(multi-turn LLM sub-dialogs), plus the two variable-substitution syntaxes
(`{{ jinja }}` and `{{uuid:Variable:<uuid>}}`) used inside speak content.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `custom_prompt_exit` | `custom_prompt until exit_criteria_met` |
| `custom_prompt_max_turns` | `custom_prompt force-advance on max_turns` |
| `dynamic_prompt_uses_api_customer_name_from_state` | `speak exact "Am I speaking with {{ customer_first_name }}?"` |
| `say_exactly_greeting` | `speak exact greeting on entry node` |
| `say_exactly_jinja` | `speak exact with {{ jinja }} from initial_state` |
| `say_exactly_node_transition` | `speak exact fires on non-entry node after edge` |
| `say_exactly_python_code` | `speak exact after collect â†’ "Got it, {{ var }}"` |
| `speak_flexible_irrelevant_answer` | `speak flexible re-ask on irrelevant answer (no duplicate)` |
| `speak_flexible_prompt_style` | `speak flexible with {{uuid:Variable:...}} prompt-style` |
| `uuid_ref_speak_exact` | `speak exact "Thank you, {{uuid:Variable:{var}}}"` |
| `uuid_ref_speak_flexible` | `speak flexible "{{uuid:Variable:{var}}}" + goodbye ref` |

results.title: steps-endcall
results.description: `end_conversation step â€” exact | flexible | jinja goodbye`
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” End Call

Exercises the `end_conversation` instruction step inside a task-following
LLMNode. The step terminates the conversation after emitting a goodbye
message, whose surface form is controlled by `goodbye_message_mode` and
optional Jinja templating against runtime state.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `deterministic_end_call` | `collect â†’ {var} then end_conversation with exact goodbye` |
| `deterministic_end_call_jinja` | `end_conversation with "{{ runtime_var }}" jinja goodbye` |
| `end_conversation` | `collect {var}Ă—3 then end_conversation with exact goodbye` |
| `llm_guided_end_call` | `end_conversation with flexible goodbye` |

results.title: steps-confirmation
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Confirmation Modes

Exercises the `confirmation_mode` field on a collected variable inside a
task-following LLM node. After a value is proposed by the user, the runtime
must either gate saving behind an explicit yes/no turn (`explicit`) or
accept on the next natural turn (`implicit`), including repair when the
user rejects and supplies a new value.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `explicit_bare_no_then_correct` | `collect â†’ {var} [explicit] â†’ user "No" â†’ user "{val}" â†’ save` |
| `explicit_confirm_repair` | `collect â†’ {var} [explicit] â†’ user "No, it's actually {val}" â†’ save corrected` |
| `explicit_confirm_yes` | `collect â†’ {var} [explicit] â†’ user "Yes" â†’ save` |
| `greeting_then_confirm_boolean` | `collect â†’ {bool} â†’ greeting must not fabricate â†’ confirm â†’ route by is_true` |
| `implicit_accepted` | `collect â†’ {var} [implicit] â†’ save on first parse` |

results.title: steps-collection-typevalidation
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Collection & Type Validation

Exercises single-node `collect` instruction steps against typed
`node_variables` (`Email`, `PhoneNumber`, `Date`, `Time`, `String`).
Each scenario pins one typing / normalization / constraint knob on the
variable and asserts that the extractor either accepts the first user
turn or rejects it and re-prompts until a valid value arrives.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `custom_validation_rejects_single_name_then_accepts_full_name` | `collect â†’ customer_name (String, custom rule in description) retry` |
| `date_must_be_future` | `collect â†’ appointment_date (Date, min_date=today) accepted` |
| `date_must_be_within_30_days` | `collect â†’ appointment_date (Date, max_date=today+30d) retry` |
| `date_must_be_within_window` | `collect â†’ appointment_date (Date, min_date=fixed 2025-06-01) accept` |
| `disallowed_domain_rejected_then_corrected` | `collect â†’ user_email (Email, allowed_domains=gmail.com) reject then accept` |
| `invalid_format_rejected_then_corrected` | `collect â†’ user_email (Email) reject invalid format then accept` |
| `invalid_phone_rejected` | `collect â†’ user_phone (PhoneNumber) reject too-short then accept` |
| `misheard_domain_corrected` | `collect â†’ user_email (Email, aggressive_correction) jmail â†’ gmail` |
| `misheard_domain_tld_preserved` | `collect â†’ user_email (Email, aggressive_correction) jmail.co.uk â†’ gmail.co.uk` |
| `phone_with_country_code` | `collect â†’ user_phone (PhoneNumber) accept explicit +1 country code` |
| `return_date_must_follow_departure` | `collect â†’ return_date (Date, min_date=relative_var departure_date+1) accept` |
| `unclear_input_reprompts_user` | `collect â†’ appointment_time (Time) retry on unclear input` |
| `valid_date_is_accepted` | `collect â†’ appointment_date (Date) accepted on first turn` |
| `valid_email_collected` | `collect â†’ user_email (Email) accepted on first turn` |
| `valid_phone_collected` | `collect â†’ user_phone (PhoneNumber, default_country_code=+1) accepted` |
| `valid_time_is_accepted` | `collect â†’ appointment_time (Time) accepted on first turn` |

results.title: steps-collection-str
results.description: `collect â†’ {var} with String / Alphanumeric / Time validation`
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” String & Code Validation

Exercises single-node `collect` steps that pin a `node_variable` to a
non-numeric type (`String`, `Alphanumeric`, `Time`) and hands validation
off to server-side constraints. Every scenario uses the same shape:

```
CollectionNode
   #1 đź—Ł speak  "Hello! ..."
   #2 đź“Ą collect â†’ <var>
        â”‚
        â–Ľ
ConfirmationNode (EndCallNode)
```

Scenarios only differ in which constraint is set on the variable and
whether the first user reply passes validation.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `code_must_match_required_pattern` | `collect â†’ {booking_code} (Alphanumeric, regex_pattern=^[A-Z]{2}\d{4}$)` |
| `code_must_meet_minimum_length` | `collect â†’ {booking_code} (Alphanumeric, min_length=6)` |
| `hyphens_accepted_with_special_chars_flag` | `collect â†’ {booking_code} (Alphanumeric, allow_special_chars=true)` |
| `max_length_exceeded_is_rejected` | `collect â†’ {booking_code} (Alphanumeric, max_length=6)` |
| `non_alphanumeric_input_is_rejected` | `collect â†’ {booking_code} rejects non-alphanumeric chars` |
| `special_chars_at_sign_still_rejected` | `collect â†’ {booking_code} rejects @ even with allow_special_chars` |
| `string_basic` | `collect â†’ {customer_name} (String, unconstrained)` |
| `string_length_violation` | `collect â†’ {reference_code} (String, min_length=4 max_length=8)` |
| `time_must_be_after_minimum` | `collect â†’ {appointment_time} (Time, min_time=09:00:00)` |
| `time_must_be_before_maximum` | `collect â†’ {appointment_time} (Time, max_time=17:00:00)` |
| `valid_code_is_accepted` | `collect â†’ {booking_code} (Alphanumeric, unconstrained)` |

results.title: steps-collection-dynamicoptions
results.description: `collect â†’ {var} from dynamic options with strict validation`
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Collection with Dynamic Options

Exercises `collect` steps whose target variable is bound to a
**dynamically-loaded option list** (`is_dynamic_options: true`,
`dynamic_options_path: "api.available_slots"`) with `strict_validation`.
The LLM is told not to invent values â€” it must pass exactly a canonical
option through the validation function, or the turn is rejected and the
caller re-prompted.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `invalid_option_is_rejected_then_corrected` | `collect â†’ {var} rejects out-of-list value, accepts canonical retry` |
| `option_description_guides_llm` | `collect â†’ {var} rejects paraphrase, requires canonical option` |

results.title: steps-collection-core
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Collection Core

Exercises the core `instruction_steps` pipeline inside a single
`task_following` LLM node: exact/flexible `speak`, deterministic
`set_value` / `bulk_set_value` auto-advance, and `collect` with typed
node variables. These are the building blocks every downstream suite
relies on.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `bulk_set_value` | `speak â†’ bulk_set_value [var=val, â€¦] â†’ collect` |
| `forced_extraction_numeric` | `collect â†’ {PhoneNumber} â€” greeting must not fabricate extraction` |
| `forced_extraction_string` | `collect â†’ {String} â€” greeting must not fabricate extraction` |
| `multi_step_collect` | `speak â†’ collect â†’ speak â†’ collect` |
| `multi_type_backend_defaults` | `collect {Float\|NumericCode\|PhoneNumber} with backend defaults` |
| `set_value_with_collect` | `speak â†’ set_value â†’ collect â†’ speak â†’ collect` |
| `simple_collect` | `speak exact â†’ collect â†’ {customer_name}` |
| `string_with_backend_defaults` | `collect â†’ {String} with confirmation_mode=explicit` |
| `type_coercion_set_value` | `set_value â†’ {bool\|int\|float} â†’ edge if [cond]` |

results.title: steps-collection-callerintent
results.description: `collect â†’ {var} with enum options (CallerIntent / String / dynamic)`
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Caller Intent & Options

Exercises `collect` steps whose target variable is constrained to a closed
option set. Covers `CallerIntent` enums, `String` enums with option
`description` fields, fuzzy matching, `is_supported: false` gating, the
catch-all `other` option, and dynamically-loaded option lists.

Every scenario uses a single `task_following` LLM entry node wired to an
`EndCallNode` confirmation step. The decision surface under test is purely
the collect validation function â€” never edge routing, never condition steps.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `description_aids_selection` | `collect â†’ {var} matched via option description, not literal value` |
| `greeting_then_enum` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} refuses to fabricate on greeting-only input` |
| `handled_intent_chosen_over_other` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} picks handled option over {other}` |
| `intent_accepted_with_fuzzy_match` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} with allow_fuzzy_match above threshold` |
| `other_chosen_for_unrelated_input` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} = {other} for unrelated, never spoken` |
| `other_intent_not_exposed_to_caller` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} never leaks {other} token on re-ask` |
| `unhandled_intent_chosen_over_other` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} rejects unhandled, does not map to {other}` |
| `unrecognized_input_is_rejected` | `collect â†’ {var} rejects off-list input, variable stays unset` |
| `unsupported_intent_is_rejected` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} rejects is_supported:false, caller corrects` |
| `valid_intent_is_accepted` | `collect â†’ {caller_intent} exact match on supported enum` |
| `valid_option_is_accepted` | `collect â†’ {var} from dynamic_options_path` |

results.title: steps-branching
results.description: In-node branching with `condition` steps â€” AND / OR / ELSE routing inside a single task-following node.
results.description_long:
# Steps â€” Condition Branching

Exercises in-node branching using `condition` instruction steps inside a
single task-following LLM node. A `condition` step evaluates one or more
condition groups against node variables and jumps (`then_goto_step`) to a
later step, with an optional `is_else` group as the fallback.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `condition_branching` | `if [cond] then goto {step}` |
| `condition_or_logic` | `if [cond] OR [cond] then goto {step}` |
| `condition_or_no_match` | `if [cond] OR [cond] else goto {step}` |

results.title: speech-formatting
results.description: `collect â†’ {var} : parse spoken {type} into canonical value`
results.description_long:
# Speech Formatting

Exercises spoken-input parsing across every scalar `node_variable` type.
Each scenario is a single task-following `LLMNode` with one `speak` step
and one `collect` step. The user turn is a naturally spoken phrase
(words, ASR artifacts, 12h time, etc.) and the assertion pins the
canonical parsed value the variable must land on.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `alphanumeric_special_chars` | `collect â†’ {var} : Alphanumeric with special chars` |
| `boolean_natural_language` | `collect â†’ {var} : Boolean from natural language` |
| `date_spoken_format` | `collect â†’ {var} : spoken date â†’ ISO 8601` |
| `email_asr_correction` | `collect â†’ {var} : Email with ASR repair` |
| `float_spoken_words` | `collect â†’ {var} : spoken words â†’ Float` |
| `integer_spoken_words` | `collect â†’ {var} : spoken words â†’ Integer` |
| `numeric_code_prompt_injection` | `collect â†’ {var} : NumericCode resists prompt injection` |
| `numeric_code_spoken_words` | `collect â†’ {var} : spoken words â†’ NumericCode digits` |
| `time_12h_to_24h` | `collect â†’ {var} : 12h spoken time â†’ 24h` |

results.title: routing
results.description: `route caller â†’ {node} via rule | prompt edges`
results.description_long:
# Routing â€” Workflow Traversal

Exercises multi-node workflow traversal and edge resolution. A caller enters
an entrypoint `LLMNode`, runs its `instruction_steps`, then an edge selects
the next node. Edges are either **rule-based** (deterministic `eq` on a
variable) or **prompt-based** (runtime picks using edge `conditional_description`).
Also covers plain linear chains and in-node `transfer` steps.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `conditional_routing_goes_to_premium_branch` | `edge: if [cond] then goto {node}` |
| `conditional_routing_goes_to_standard_branch` | `edge: is_else â†’ goto StandardEnd` |
| `multi_node_linear` | `node1 â†’ node2 â†’ node3 â†’ end (linear edges)` |
| `multi_node_transfer` | `greet â†’ triage â†’ transfer to {phone}` |
| `prompt_based_multi_edge` | `edge: prompt-based pick {node} from {intent}` |
| `prompt_based_uncertain_intent` | `edge: prompt-based fallback â†’ GeneralSupport on unclear {intent}` |
| `transfer_step` | `collect â†’ {var} then transfer to {phone}` |

results.title: output-formatting
results.description_long:
# Output Formatting

Exercises how collected variables are transformed before persistence and
how they are referenced from speech / goodbye messages. Covers three
knobs on a node variable plus the runtime UUID reference syntax:

- `output_template` â€” Jinja filter applied to the raw extracted value
- `output_format` â€” post-template cast (e.g. `integer`)
- `{{uuid:Variable:<uuid>}}` â€” runtime substitution inside `goodbye_message`
- Sequential `collect` steps referenced by `variable_name`

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `combined_format_and_template` | `collect â†’ {var} \| output_template \| output_format:integer` |
| `output_format_integer` | `collect â†’ {var} \| output_format:integer` |
| `output_template_upper` | `collect â†’ {var} \| output_template:{{ value \| upper }}` |
| `ref_by_name` | `collect â†’ {var1} â†’ {var2} â†’ {var3}  (by variable_name)` |
| `ref_mixed` | `collect â†’ {String} â†’ speak â†’ {String} â†’ {Boolean}` |
| `uuid_ref_goodbye_message` | `end_conversation "... {{uuid:Variable:{uuid}}} ..." (exact)` |
| `value_transformation_saves_title_cased_name` | `collect â†’ {var} \| output_template:{{ value \| title }}` |

results.title: edges-routing
results.description: Between-node routing via rule-based edges â€” first-match-wins ordering with an `is_else` fallback edge.
results.description_long:
# Edges â€” Rule-Based Routing

Exercises **between-node** routing using rule-based edges. After a
classification node finishes, the workflow walks its outbound edges in
`order` and takes the first edge whose `condition_groups` match. An
`is_else: true` edge serves as the fallback when nothing matches.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `rule_based_and_conditions` | `edge: if [cond AND cond] then goto {node}` |
| `rule_based_branching` | `edge: if [cond] then goto {node}` |
| `rule_based_else_fallback` | `edge: is_else â†’ goto {node}` |

results.title: advanced-escalation
results.description_long:
# Agent Escalation

**Use-case:** caller-requested human agent escalation

**Template:** `escalation: counter / scripts / disabled / resume`

The runtime watches every caller turn for an "I want a human" intent. When
detected, it consults the workflow's top-level `escalation_config` and either
pushes back, transfers, or ignores â€” depending on counter, mode, and whether
escalation is enabled at all.

## Scenarios

| scenario | template |
|---|---|
| `agent_request_counter_pushback` | `escalation: counter=N flexible â†’ pushback Ă— N then transfer` |
| `agent_request_custom_prompt_resume` | `escalation: counter=1 â†’ pushback during custom_prompt â†’ resume` |
| `agent_request_disabled` | `escalation: null â†’ bot handles agent request as plain text` |
| `agent_request_exact_scripts` | `escalation: counter=N exact â†’ deliver scripts[0..N-1] then transfer` |
| `agent_request_immediate_transfer` | `escalation: counter=0 â†’ transfer on first request` |
| `agent_request_mid_step_resume` | `escalation: counter=1 â†’ pushback mid-collect â†’ resume same step` |
